description: Your BMI is too low. Focus on supplements that support healthy weight gain, muscle building, and nutrient absorption.

medicines.name: Zinc Supplement
medicines.benefit: Improves appetite, boosts immune function, and supports protein synthesis for muscle gain
medicines.warning: Do not exceed 40mg/day. Take with food to avoid nausea.

medicines.name: Vitamin B12
medicines.benefit: Boosts energy metabolism, improves appetite, and supports red blood cell production
medicines.warning: Safe for most people. Consult a doctor if you have kidney issues.

medicines.name: Protein Powder (Whey)
medicines.benefit: Rapidly increases protein intake, supports muscle repair and healthy weight gain
medicines.warning: Avoid if lactose intolerant. Choose plant-based alternatives if needed.

medicines.name: Iron Supplement
medicines.benefit: Prevents anemia, improves energy levels, and supports healthy blood production
medicines.warning: Take on empty stomach for better absorption. May cause constipation.

medicines.name: Multivitamin Complex
medicines.benefit: Fills nutritional gaps, improves overall health and energy for weight gain journey
medicines.warning: Do not exceed recommended dose. Store in a cool dry place.

medicines.name: Omega-3 Fish Oil
medicines.benefit: Supports muscle growth, reduces inflammation, and improves nutrient absorption
medicines.warning: May interact with blood thinners. Consult a doctor if on medication.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squats
exercises.benefit: Builds lower body muscle mass, increases overall body weight through muscle

exercises.name: Push-Ups
exercises.benefit: Builds chest, shoulders and triceps — key muscle groups for upper body mass

exercises.name: Deadlifts
exercises.benefit: Full body compound movement that stimulates maximum muscle growth

exercises.name: Bench Press
exercises.benefit: Targets chest and shoulders to build a wider, stronger upper body frame

exercises.name: Light Jogging
exercises.benefit: Improves cardiovascular health without burning too many calories

exercises.name: Plank Hold
exercises.benefit: Builds core stability which is essential to support heavy compound lifts

exerciseTips: Focus on strength training over cardio — your goal is to gain, not burn
Rest at least 48 hours between training the same muscle group
Eat a protein-rich meal or shake within 30 minutes after every workout
Sleep 7–9 hours — muscle is built during sleep, not during the workout
Increase weight gradually each week to progressively overload muscles
medicineTips: Always consult a doctor before starting any supplement regimen
Take supplements with food unless the label says otherwise
Stay consistent — supplements take 4–8 weeks to show noticeable results
Supplements are an addition to food, not a replacement for real meals
doctorNote: If you have been underweight for more than 3 months, please consult a doctor to rule out underlying conditions such as thyroid disorders, anemia, or digestive issues.

description: Your BMI is healthy. Focus on supplements that maintain your health, energy, and immunity.

medicines.name: Vitamin D3
medicines.dosage: 1000–2000 IU daily with a fatty meal
medicines.benefit: Supports bone health, immune system, and mood regulation for overall wellbeing
medicines.warning: Do not take more than 4000 IU per day without a doctor's advice.

medicines.name: Vitamin C
medicines.benefit: Powerful antioxidant that boosts immunity, improves skin health, and fights free radicals
medicines.warning: High doses (2000mg+) may cause digestive discomfort.

medicines.name: Probiotic Capsules
medicines.dosage: 1 capsule daily on an empty stomach
medicines.benefit: Maintains healthy gut flora, improves digestion, and strengthens the immune response
medicines.warning: Refrigerate after opening. Not recommended during antibiotic treatment.

medicines.name: Magnesium
medicines.benefit: Improves sleep quality, reduces muscle cramps, and supports nervous system function
medicines.warning: High doses may cause loose stools. Start with a lower dose.

medicines.name: Omega-3 Fish Oil
medicines.benefit: Supports heart health, brain function, and reduces inflammation throughout the body
medicines.warning: May interact with blood thinners. Consult doctor if on medication.

medicines.name: Biotin (Vitamin B7)
medicines.benefit: Promotes healthy hair, strong nails, and supports energy metabolism
medicines.warning: May interfere with thyroid lab tests. Inform your doctor if getting blood work.

exercises.name: Brisk Walking
exercises.benefit: Maintains cardiovascular health, controls weight, and boosts daily energy levels

exercises.name: Yoga
exercises.benefit: Improves flexibility, reduces stress, and enhances mental clarity and focus

exercises.name: Swimming
exercises.benefit: Full body workout that tones muscles, burns calories, and is easy on the joints

exercises.name: Cycling
exercises.benefit: Strengthens legs, improves stamina, and is great for maintaining a healthy weight

exercises.name: Bodyweight Circuit
exercises.benefit: Maintains muscle tone, burns calories, and improves overall functional fitness

exercises.name: Stretching & Mobility
exercises.benefit: Prevents injury, relieves muscle tension, and keeps joints healthy and flexible

exerciseTips: Aim for a mix of cardio and strength training each week
Consistency beats intensity — 30 minutes daily is better than 2 hours once a week
Warm up for 5 minutes before every exercise session
Cool down and stretch for 5–10 minutes after every workout
Listen to your body — rest when you feel pain, not just soreness
medicineTips: You don't need heavy supplements — basic vitamins and minerals are enough
Get a blood test annually to check for any deficiencies
A well-balanced diet should cover most nutritional needs
Consult a doctor before adding any new supplement to your routine
doctorNote: Your BMI is in the healthy range. An annual check-up is recommended to monitor blood pressure, cholesterol, and blood sugar levels.

description: Your BMI is above the healthy range. Use supplements that support fat metabolism and exercises that burn calories efficiently.

medicines.name: Green Tea Extract
medicines.benefit: Naturally boosts metabolism, increases fat burning, and provides antioxidant support
medicines.warning: Contains caffeine. Avoid if sensitive to caffeine or if you have heart issues.

medicines.name: CLA (Conjugated Linoleic Acid)
medicines.benefit: Helps reduce body fat while preserving lean muscle mass during weight loss
medicines.warning: May cause mild digestive upset initially. Take with food.

medicines.name: Fiber Supplement (Psyllium Husk)
medicines.dosage: 5–10g mixed in water, 30 min before meals
medicines.benefit: Reduces appetite by creating a feeling of fullness, helps control calorie intake
medicines.warning: Always take with plenty of water. Can cause bloating if you don't drink enough.

medicines.name: Vitamin D3
medicines.dosage: 2000 IU daily with a fatty meal
medicines.benefit: Deficiency is linked to weight gain. Supplementing helps regulate fat storage hormones
medicines.warning: Do not exceed 4000 IU per day without doctor guidance.

medicines.name: Apple Cider Vinegar Capsules
medicines.benefit: Helps control blood sugar spikes after meals and supports gradual fat loss
medicines.warning: May interact with diabetes medications. Consult a doctor if diabetic.

medicines.name: Chromium Picolinate
medicines.benefit: Reduces sugar cravings, improves insulin sensitivity, and supports healthy metabolism
medicines.warning: Consult a doctor if you have diabetes or kidney disease.

exercises.name: Power Walking
exercises.benefit: Burns significant calories, easy on joints, and very effective for consistent fat loss

exercises.name: Jump Rope
exercises.benefit: Burns up to 400 calories in 20 minutes, improves coordination and cardiovascular fitness

exercises.name: Water Aerobics
exercises.benefit: Burns calories effectively with no stress on joints — ideal for overweight individuals

exercises.name: Stationary Bike
exercises.benefit: Low impact but high calorie burn — great for beginners starting a weight loss journey

exercises.name: Resistance Band Training
exercises.benefit: Builds lean muscle that increases resting metabolic rate, burning more fat 24/7

exercises.name: HIIT (Beginner)
exercises.benefit: Maximum calorie burn in minimum time, keeps metabolism elevated for hours after

exerciseTips: Start slow and gradually increase intensity each week
Cardio should be your main focus — aim for 300+ minutes per week
Never exercise on an empty stomach — eat a light snack 30 min before
Track your steps — aim for at least 10,000 steps every day
Pair exercise with a calorie deficit diet for best results
medicineTips: Supplements alone will not cause weight loss — they only support your diet and exercise
Avoid unregulated 'fat burner' pills — they can be dangerous
Get your thyroid checked — an underactive thyroid can cause weight gain
Consult a doctor before taking any weight loss supplement
doctorNote: If you have been unable to lose weight despite diet and exercise for 3+ months, consult a doctor to check for hormonal imbalances such as hypothyroidism or PCOS.

description: Your BMI indicates obesity. Medical supervision is strongly recommended alongside lifestyle changes.
warning: A BMI over 30 carries serious health risks. Please consult a certified doctor before starting any supplement or exercise program.

medicines.name: Metformin (Prescription Only)
medicines.benefit: Improves insulin sensitivity, helps manage blood sugar, and supports weight management in obese individuals
medicines.warning: PRESCRIPTION REQUIRED. Never take without a doctor's prescription. Regular monitoring required.

medicines.name: Berberine
medicines.benefit: Natural alternative that improves insulin sensitivity, reduces blood sugar, and supports weight loss
medicines.warning: Consult a doctor before use. May interact with diabetes medications.

medicines.name: Fiber Supplement (High Dose)
medicines.benefit: Controls hunger significantly, slows glucose absorption, and supports healthy digestion
medicines.warning: Increase water intake substantially. Introduce gradually to avoid gas.

medicines.name: Vitamin D3 + K2
medicines.benefit: Obesity commonly causes vitamin D deficiency. This combination supports bone and heart health
medicines.warning: High doses require doctor monitoring. Get blood levels checked every 3 months.

medicines.name: Coenzyme Q10 (CoQ10)
medicines.dosage: 100–200 mg daily with a fatty meal
medicines.benefit: Improves cellular energy production, supports heart health which is vital for obese individuals
medicines.warning: May lower blood pressure. Consult doctor if on blood pressure medication.

medicines.name: Omega-3 (High Dose)
medicines.benefit: Reduces inflammation associated with obesity, supports heart and liver health
medicines.warning: High doses require medical supervision. May interact with blood thinners.

exercises.name: Gentle Walking
exercises.benefit: Safe starting point for obese individuals — burns calories without joint stress

exercises.name: Chair Exercises
exercises.benefit: Strengthens muscles with zero joint impact — ideal for those with mobility limitations

exercises.name: Water Walking
exercises.benefit: Water supports body weight reducing joint stress while burning significant calories

exercises.name: Seated Yoga
exercises.benefit: Improves flexibility, reduces stress (which causes weight gain), and builds body awareness

exercises.name: Wall Push-Ups
exercises.benefit: Builds upper body strength safely without putting pressure on wrists or knees

exercises.name: Deep Breathing Exercise
exercises.benefit: Reduces cortisol (stress hormone) which directly contributes to belly fat accumulation

exerciseTips: Start with just 10–15 minutes daily and increase by 5 minutes each week
Choose low-impact exercises to protect your joints from excess pressure
Never exercise through pain — discomfort is okay, sharp pain is not
Work with a certified physiotherapist or trainer if possible
Consistency over intensity — small daily movement is more powerful than occasional intense sessions
medicineTips: Do not take any weight loss medicine without a doctor's prescription
Obesity is a medical condition — treat it as seriously as any other disease
Get regular blood tests to monitor blood sugar, cholesterol, and liver function
Prescription weight loss medications like Orlistat or GLP-1 agonists may be appropriate — ask your doctor
doctorNote: With a BMI over 30, you are at significantly higher risk for Type 2 Diabetes, Heart Disease, and Hypertension. Please consult a doctor immediately for a comprehensive health plan.
